company.what_they_do: PunkProof operates a public email service focused on spam prevention and platform safety. It provides email addresses to users (e.g. alice012@punkproof.com) but explicitly does not allow sending messages to the internet. It positions itself as a legitimate, non-throwaway email service and asks not to be blocklisted, suggesting it serves as a shared/community inbox or alias email provider. It includes an abuse reporting system.
company.raw_notes:
- Public email service, not a throwaway/disposable email provider (they self-assert this)
- Primary goal: prevent spam, maintain safe/secure environment
- Does NOT allow sending messages to the internet (inbound/alias only, or internal only)
- Strictly prohibits illegal activities
- Requests domain not be blocked by receiving mail servers
- Suggests site owners ban problematic users by IP rather than blocking the domain
- Has an abuse ticket system for reporting
- Appears to be a relatively new or low-profile service (noted as potentially too new for web search indexing)
- The org_name 'Alicesec' does not match 'PunkProof' — likely a personal/informal label entered by the user at signup, not an actual separate company

individual.name: Alice
individual.notes: Only the first name 'Alice' is known. The org_name entered at signup is 'Alicesec', which may be a personal handle or informal label rather than a registered business. No role, title, LinkedIn, or other individual-level information was findable. The email username 'alice012' suggests this may be one of multiple Alice-named accounts on the platform, implying a shared/public service context. No verified employer, founder status, or professional background can be asserted.
